scope: Pass B full-surface expansion. Clarifai's public documentation site (docs.clarifai.com) is a gRPC-first SDK-usage guide with no single browsable REST endpoint index (unlike bitly/clickup's OpenAPI-backed reference sites) -- its REST surface had to be reconstructed from Clarifai's own published REST convention article (clarifai.com/blog/clarifai-api-conventions), its dataset/app lifecycle guides (docs.clarifai.com/api-guide/data/*, docs.clarifai.com/create/applications/*), and confirmed curl examples for the specific endpoints implemented below. New in this pass: create_application, update_application, create_dataset, delete_dataset write actions (each grounded in a directly-confirmed request-body example, cited in docs.md); capabilities.write flips true. Every other resource category (models, model versions, workflows, inputs, concepts, predictions, training, annotations) is excluded below with a specific reason -- prediction/training/annotation endpoints require deep per-resource body shapes (image/text payload encoding, concept-vector bodies) this pass did not independently re-verify to the same confidence bar as the 4 implemented actions, so they are excluded as out_of_scope/requires_elevated_scope rather than guessed at.

excluded.reason: single-application detail; identical record shape to the applications list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

irreversibly deletes an application and every dataset/model/workflow/input it contains; no independently-confirmed exact request-body shape (ids vs app_ids vs delete_all) was found to the same confidence bar as this pass's 4 implemented writes, and the blast radius (whole-app data loss) makes a best-guess shape unacceptable to ship

dataset PATCH (rename/re-describe/set default_filter_id via action=merge|overwrite) is a lower-value near-duplicate of update_application's already-covered action+plural-array PATCH convention; deferred as breadth-vs-cost triage
single-dataset detail; identical record shape to the datasets list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
dataset version history (snapshot metadata for training), not part of legacy's parity surface; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
creates a dataset training snapshot, not a plain data-object mutation; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage

input/annotation ingestion requires an image/text/video payload (URL, base64, or raw bytes) and concept-label bodies with a shape this pass did not independently re-verify to the same confidence bar as the 4 implemented writes; not modeled by legacy either
single-model detail; identical record shape to the models list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
legacy connector never implemented model creation; the real body (model_type_id, output_info.data.concepts) was not independently re-verified this pass; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
prediction/inference endpoint (runs a model against an input and returns scores), not a syncable object stream or plain data mutation
prediction/inference endpoint pinned to a specific model version, not a syncable object stream or plain data mutation
kicks off asynchronous model training, not a plain data mutation; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
async training-status polling, not a syncable object stream
single-workflow detail; identical record shape to the workflows list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
workflow creation requires a nodes/model-linking DSL body this pass did not independently re-verify; legacy never implemented it; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
workflow inference endpoint (runs a workflow's chained models against an input), not a syncable object stream or plain data mutation
concept (label taxonomy) listing, not part of legacy's parity surface; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
concept create/update via action=merge|overwrite, not part of legacy's parity surface; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
raw uploaded-input listing (images/text/video), not part of legacy's parity surface; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
bulk input/annotation update requires the same payload/concept-body shape as input creation, not independently re-verified this pass
bulk deletion of uploaded inputs and their annotations; exact request-body shape (ids vs delete_all) not independently re-verified to the confidence bar this pass requires for a destructive action
